briefdescription.para: linear element accessor (treats the field as a vector); no bounds check
linear element accessor (treats the field as a vector); no bounds check
element accessor; no bounds check

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword row, const uword col) const
memberdef.name: at

briefdescription.para: element accessor; no bounds check
creation of subview_field (column of a field)
creation of subview_field (column of a field)
creation of subview_field (subfield comprised of specified columns)

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword in_col1, const uword in_col2) const
memberdef.name: cols

briefdescription.para: creation of subview_field (subfield comprised of specified columns)
change the field to have the specified dimensions (data is not preserved)
construct a field from a given field
construct a field from subview_field (e.g. construct a field from a delayed subfield operation)

detaileddescription.para: construct the field with the specified number of elements, assuming a column-major layout

briefdescription.para: construct the field with the specified dimensions
fill the field with an object
returns true if the given index is currently in range
returns true if the given start and end indices are currently in range

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword in_row, const uword in_col) const
memberdef.name: in_range

briefdescription.para: returns true if the given location is currently in range
construct a field from a given field
internal field construction; if the requested size is small enough, memory from the stack is used. otherwise memory is allocated via 'new'
returns true if the field has no objects

briefdescription.para: linear element accessor (treats the field as a vector); bounds checking not done when ARMA_NO_DEBUG is defined
linear element accessor (treats the field as a vector); bounds checking not done when ARMA_NO_DEBUG is defined
element accessor; bounds checking not done when ARMA_NO_DEBUG is defined

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword row, const uword col) const
memberdef.name: operator()

briefdescription.para: element accessor; bounds checking not done when ARMA_NO_DEBUG is defined
construct a field from a given field
construct a field from subview_field (e.g. construct a field from a delayed subfield operation)
linear element accessor (treats the field as a vector); no bounds check
linear element accessor (treats the field as a vector); no bounds check

detaileddescription.para: print contents of the field (to the cout stream), optionally preceding with a user specified line of text. the field class preserves the stream's flags but the associated operator<< function for type oT may still modify the stream's parameters. NOTE: this function assumes that type oT can be printed, i.e. the function "std::ostream& operator<< (std::ostream&, const oT&)" has been defined.

print contents of the field to a user specified stream, optionally preceding with a user specified line of text. the field class preserves the stream's flags but the associated operator<< function for type oT may still modify the stream's parameters. NOTE: this function assumes that type oT can be printed, i.e. the function "std::ostream& operator<< (std::ostream&, const oT&)" has been defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string name, const file_type type=arma_binary) const
memberdef.name: quiet_save

briefdescription.para: reset the field to an empty state (i.e. the field will have no objects)
reset each object
